trivia.name: Trivia
trivia.summary: Test your knowledge across multiple categories!
rules: A question is shown with 4 multiple-choice answers
Answer as fast as you can — faster answers earn more points
The host can pick a category or play all randomly
After each question, scores are shown on the leaderboard
Base score is 500 points, scaled by how quickly you answer
trivia.tips: Read the full question before answering — accuracy beats speed!

wordscramble.name: Word Scramble
wordscramble.summary: Unscramble jumbled letters to reveal the hidden word!
rules: A word is scrambled and shown as jumbled letters
Type your guess in the input box and submit
Faster correct answers earn more points
Hints may appear if no one guesses in time
Categories can be selected for themed words
wordscramble.tips: Look for common letter patterns like -tion, -ing, or -ness.

speedmath.name: Speed Math
speedmath.summary: Solve math equations as fast as possible!
rules: A math equation appears (addition, subtraction, or multiplication)
Type the correct answer as a number
First correct answer gets the most points (500), each subsequent correct answer gets less
Problems get harder as the game progresses
If time runs out, no points are awarded
speedmath.tips: For multiplication, try breaking numbers into easier parts (e.g., 15×12 = 15×10 + 15×2).

emoji.name: Emoji Decoder
emoji.summary: Guess the movie, phrase, or thing from emoji clues!
rules: A series of emojis represents a movie, phrase, or concept
Type your guess — it must match closely (minor differences OK)
Faster correct guesses earn more points
Hints are given gradually if no one guesses correctly
Categories include movies, foods, animals, and more
emoji.tips: Think about what each emoji literally represents and how they connect.

drawguess.name: Draw & Guess
drawguess.summary: One player draws, everyone else guesses the word!
rules: The drawer picks a word from 3 choices
Using the canvas, draw the word — NO letters or numbers allowed
Other players type guesses in the chat
Correct guessers AND the drawer earn points
Earlier correct guesses earn more points for both guesser and drawer
Each player takes turns drawing
drawguess.tips: Start with the basic shape/outline, then add details. Simple drawings are often clearest!

codenames.name: Codenames
codenames.summary: Give one-word clues to help your team find their agents!
rules: Split into two teams: Red and Blue
Each team has a Spymaster who sees which words belong to which team
Spymasters give a one-word clue plus a number (how many words it relates to)
Team members tap words they think match the clue
Avoid the assassin word — touching it loses the game instantly!
First team to find all their words wins
codenames.tips: As Spymaster, find connections between multiple words for bigger combos.

colorclash.name: Color Clash
colorclash.summary: Match colors or numbers to get rid of your cards first!
rules: Each player gets 7 cards with colors and numbers
Match the top card by color OR number to play a card
Special cards: Skip, Reverse, Draw Two, Wild, Wild Draw Four
If you can't play, draw a card from the deck
First player to empty their hand wins the round
Call 'Clash!' when you have one card left
colorclash.tips: Save wild cards for when you're stuck. Try to change to a color you have most of.

blackjack.name: Blackjack
blackjack.summary: Get as close to 21 as possible without going over!
rules: Place your bet at the start of each round
You're dealt 2 cards — try to get as close to 21 as possible
Number cards = face value, Face cards = 10, Ace = 1 or 11
Hit to draw another card, Stand to keep your hand
Going over 21 is a 'bust' — you lose your bet
Beat the dealer's hand to win double your bet
blackjack.tips: Stand on 17 or higher. Hit on 11 or lower. Be cautious with 12-16.

hangman.name: Hangman
hangman.summary: Guess the hidden word one letter at a time before running out of chances!
rules: A word is hidden and shown as blank spaces
Players take turns guessing letters
Correct letters are revealed in their positions
Wrong guesses add a body part to the hangman (6 max)
If the full hangman is drawn, the word is revealed and the round ends
Guess the full word to earn bonus points
hangman.tips: Start with common letters: E, T, A, O, I, N, S, R.

memorymatch.name: Memory Match
memorymatch.summary: Flip cards and find matching pairs!
rules: Cards are laid face-down in a grid
On your turn, flip two cards
If they match, you keep the pair and go again
If they don't match, cards flip back and the next player goes
The player with the most pairs at the end wins
Remember where cards are to find matches faster
memorymatch.tips: Focus on remembering cards others have flipped. Watch every turn carefully!

spyfall.name: Spyfall
spyfall.summary: Find the spy — or bluff your way through if you're the spy!
rules: Everyone gets the same location card — except the spy, who gets nothing
Players take turns asking each other questions about the location
Non-spies: Answer vaguely enough that the spy can't figure out the location
The spy: Bluff and try to figure out where everyone is
At any time, players can vote on who they think the spy is
Spy wins by guessing the location OR by not being caught
spyfall.tips: As a non-spy, be specific enough to prove you know the location but vague enough to protect it.

wavelength.name: Wavelength
wavelength.summary: Guess where a clue falls on a spectrum between two extremes!
rules: A spectrum appears with two opposing concepts (e.g., Hot ↔ Cold)
The clue-giver sees where the target is on the spectrum
They give a one-word or short clue to help others guess the position
Other players position their guess on the spectrum
Points are awarded based on how close each guess is to the target
Players take turns being the clue-giver
wavelength.tips: As clue-giver, think of something that clearly sits at that specific point on the spectrum.

justone.name: Just One
justone.summary: Everyone writes a one-word clue, but duplicates are removed!
rules: One player is the guesser and cannot see the secret word
All other players write a one-word clue to help the guesser
Duplicate clues are eliminated — only unique clues are shown
The guesser sees the remaining clues and makes ONE guess
The team scores if the guesser gets it right
Each player takes a turn being the guesser
justone.tips: Avoid obvious clues that others will also write. Think of creative, unique associations!

wouldyourather.name: Would You Rather
wouldyourather.summary: Pick between two options and see what everyone else chose!
rules: Two options are presented (e.g., 'fly' vs 'be invisible')
Everyone votes for their preferred option
After all votes are in, results are revealed with percentages
You score points by being in the majority — popular opinion wins!
Voting is anonymous until the reveal
Discuss and debate after each round!
wouldyourather.tips: Think about what most people would choose, not just what you'd prefer.

wordchain.name: Word Chain
wordchain.summary: Chain words together — each word must start with the last letter of the previous word!
rules: A starting word is given
Type a word that starts with the LAST letter of the previous word
Words must be real English words (3+ letters)
No repeating words that have already been used
You have limited time for each turn — be quick!
Miss your turn or repeat a word and you're eliminated
wordchain.tips: Avoid words ending in X, Z, or Q — they're harder to chain from!

imposter.name: Imposter
imposter.summary: One player is the imposter with only a vague clue — can they survive or will the group identify them?
rules: Each round has a new secret word, a new category, and a new randomly chosen imposter
Everyone sees the word — except the imposter, who only gets a vague clue
Players take turns describing the word without saying it directly
The imposter must blend in and describe as if they know the word
After descriptions, everyone votes — a vote needs >50% majority to count
You can skip your vote if you're unsure — if >50% skip, the vote is skipped
If the vote fails (no majority, wrong person, or skip), you get another describe + vote attempt
The number of vote attempts per word is set in game settings (default: 2)
If the imposter is correctly voted out, they get one last chance to guess the word
Nobody is ejected — everyone plays all rounds regardless of vote results
Points: Imposter survives = 300pts, Imposter guesses word = 500pts, Villagers catch imposter = 200pts each
imposter.tips: As a villager, be specific enough to prove you know the word but vague enough that the imposter can't figure it out. As the imposter, listen carefully to others' descriptions to piece together the word!

ludo.name: Ludo
ludo.summary: Classic board game — roll the dice and race all 4 tokens from base to home before your opponents!
rules: Each player has 4 colored tokens starting in their base
Roll a 6 to move a token out of the base onto the starting square
Move a token forward by the number shown on the dice
Rolling a 6 gives you an extra turn (but 3 sixes in a row = lose your turn)
Land on an opponent's token to capture it and send it back to their base
Tokens on star (★) squares are safe and cannot be captured
Enter your home stretch by reaching the colored lane leading to the center
You must roll the exact number needed to reach home — no overshooting
First player to get all 4 tokens home wins (500 points!)
Capturing an opponent's token earns you a bonus turn
ludo.tips: Balance between advancing tokens and keeping them safe. Don't leave tokens on unsafe squares when opponents are close behind!

poker.name: Poker
poker.summary: Texas Hold'em — use your 2 hole cards and 5 community cards to make the best 5-card hand and win the pot!
rules: Each player starts with 1000 chips. Small blind: 10, Big blind: 20
Each player is dealt 2 private hole cards (only you can see them)
Pre-Flop: bet, call, raise or fold before the community cards are revealed
Flop: 3 community cards are revealed — another betting round follows
Turn: a 4th community card is revealed — another betting round
River: the 5th and final community card — last betting round
Showdown: remaining players reveal hands; best 5-card hand wins the pot
Hand rankings (best to worst): Royal Flush, Straight Flush, Four of a Kind, Full House, Flush, Straight, Three of a Kind, Two Pair, One Pair, High Card
You can go All-In to bet all your chips at any time
Player with all the chips (or most chips when host ends game) wins!
poker.tips: Position matters — acting last gives you more information. Don't chase bad hands. Know when to fold!

chess.name: Chess
chess.summary: The classic strategy game — checkmate your opponent's king!
rules: White moves first, then players alternate turns
Each piece type moves differently: pawns forward, rooks in straight lines, bishops diagonally, knights in an L-shape, queens combine rook + bishop, king one square any direction
Capture opponent pieces by moving to their square
The king is in 'check' when threatened — you must escape check on your turn
Checkmate occurs when the king cannot escape check — game over!
Special moves: castling (king + rook swap), en passant (pawn capture), pawn promotion (pawn reaches last rank)
Stalemate (no legal moves but not in check) results in a draw
Players can offer draws or resign at any time
chess.tips: Control the center of the board. Develop your pieces (knights and bishops) early. Castle to protect your king!

battleship.name: Battleship
battleship.summary: Deploy your fleet and sink the enemy ships before they sink yours!
rules: Each player places 5 ships on their 10×10 grid: Carrier (5), Battleship (4), Cruiser (3), Submarine (3), Destroyer (2)
Ships can be placed horizontally or vertically — no overlapping or going off the grid
Players take turns firing at coordinates on the opponent's grid
A hit is marked with 💥 and a miss with a dot
When all cells of a ship are hit, it's sunk!
First player to sink all 5 enemy ships wins
battleship.tips: Don't cluster your ships together. Use a checkerboard search pattern to find ships efficiently!

rummy.name: Rummy
rummy.summary: Form sets and runs from your cards to go out first!
rules: Each player is dealt a hand of cards from a standard 52-card deck
On your turn: draw one card (from deck or discard pile), then discard one card
Form melds: sets (3-4 cards of same rank, different suits) or runs (3+ consecutive cards of same suit)
Lay melds on the table by selecting 3+ cards and clicking 'Lay Meld'
You can also add cards to existing melds on the table
First player to empty their hand (after discarding) wins!
rummy.tips: Keep track of what opponents pick from the discard pile. Hold cards that give you multiple meld options!

coup.name: Coup
coup.summary: Bluff, deceive, and eliminate opponents in this game of political intrigue!
rules: Each player starts with 2 influence cards (face down) and 2 coins
On your turn, choose an action: Income (+1), Foreign Aid (+2), Coup (pay 7, target loses influence), or a character action
Character actions: Duke (Tax +3), Assassin (pay 3 to assassinate), Captain (steal 2 coins), Ambassador (exchange cards with deck)
Any player can challenge an action — if the actor doesn't have the claimed character, they lose influence
If a challenge fails (actor DID have the card), the challenger loses influence instead
Some characters can block: Duke blocks Foreign Aid, Contessa blocks Assassination, Captain/Ambassador block Stealing
Blocks can also be challenged!
With 10+ coins, you MUST coup — no other action allowed
Lose both influence cards and you're eliminated. Last player standing wins!
coup.tips: Bluffing is key! Claim roles you don't have, but be ready to be challenged. Watch what others claim to catch liars.

wordle.name: Wordle
wordle.summary: Guess the 5-letter word in 6 tries — compete to solve it fastest!
rules: A secret 5-letter word is chosen each round
Type a valid 5-letter word and press Enter to guess
Green 🟩 means the letter is correct and in the right position
Yellow 🟨 means the letter is in the word but in the wrong position
Gray ⬜ means the letter is not in the word at all
You have 6 guesses per round to find the word
All players play simultaneously — race to solve it first!
Multiple rounds are played with a scoreboard tracking wins
wordle.tips: Start with words that have common letters like CRANE, SLATE, or AUDIO. Eliminate vowels early!

dixit.name: Dixit
dixit.summary: A creative storytelling game — give clues about your card art and fool the others!
rules: Each player has a hand of cards with unique emoji art
The storyteller selects a card and gives a clue (word, phrase, or sentence) about it
Other players choose a card from their hand that best matches the clue
All played cards are shuffled and revealed — players vote on which card is the storyteller's
If ALL or NO players guess correctly, the storyteller gets 0 points and everyone else gets 2
If some but not all guess correctly, the storyteller and correct guessers get 3 points each
Players also get 1 bonus point for each vote on their decoy card
First player to reach the target score wins!
dixit.tips: As storyteller, be subtle — too obvious and everyone guesses right (0 points). Too obscure and no one gets it!

knowme.name: How Well Do You Know Me?
knowme.summary: Answer questions about each other and see how well you match!
rules: Both players receive the same question each round
Each player types their answer independently within the time limit
After both answer, the responses are revealed side by side
If your answers match (similar wording counts), you both earn 500 points!
At the end, you get a compatibility percentage based on how many you matched
knowme.tips: Think about what your partner would say, not just what you think! The more you know each other, the higher your score.

connectfour.name: Connect Four
connectfour.summary: Drop discs into the grid and be the first to connect four in a row!
rules: Players take turns dropping colored discs (🔴 or 🟡) into a 7-column, 6-row grid
Discs fall to the lowest available position in the chosen column
First player to connect 4 discs in a row — horizontally, vertically, or diagonally — wins!
If the board fills up with no winner, it's a draw
connectfour.tips: Control the center column! It gives you the most options for connecting four.

tictactoe.name: Tic Tac Toe
tictactoe.summary: Classic X's and O's — get three in a row to win!
rules: Players take turns placing their mark (❌ or ⭕) on a 3×3 grid
First player to get 3 marks in a row — horizontally, vertically, or diagonally — wins!
If all 9 cells are filled with no winner, it's a draw
tictactoe.tips: Always take the center if it's free. Corner openings give you the best chance to set up a fork!

partyprompts.name: Piloco
partyprompts.summary: Wild prompts, dares, and challenges for the whole group!
rules: Each round a prompt appears — it could target everyone, a specific player, or set up a versus challenge
Prompts include dares, rules, and group challenges with player names filled in
After reading the prompt, tap 'Done!' when you've completed or acknowledged it
There's no scoring — it's all about having fun and keeping the party going!
partyprompts.tips: Don't hold back! The crazier the energy, the better the party. 🎉

kingscup.name: King's Cup
kingscup.summary: Draw cards from the deck — each card has a drinking rule!
rules: Players take turns drawing a card from the deck
Each card has a rule: A=Waterfall, 2=You (pick someone), 3=Me (you drink), 4=Floor (last to touch floor drinks)
5=Guys drink, 6=Chicks drink, 7=Heaven (last to point up), 8=Mate (pick a partner), 9=Rhyme, 10=Categories
J=Make a Rule, Q=Question Master, K=Pour into King's Cup
The 4th King drawn must drink the King's Cup — game over!
kingscup.tips: Remember the active rules! The Question Master and rule cards stay in effect until the next one is drawn.

mostlikelyto.name: Most Likely To
mostlikelyto.summary: Vote on who's most likely to do something — most votes drinks!
rules: Each round shows a 'Most likely to...' scenario
Everyone votes for the player they think is most likely
After voting, results are revealed — the player with the most votes drinks!
Each vote you receive counts as 100 points (more votes = more drinks)
mostlikelyto.tips: Be honest with your votes — that's what makes it fun! Don't just target one person every time. 😏

neverhaveiever.name: Never Have I Ever
neverhaveiever.summary: Confess your experiences or stay safe — but everyone will know!
rules: Each round shows a 'Never have I ever...' statement
If you HAVE done it, tap '🍻 I Have' (you drink and get 100 points)
If you HAVEN'T done it, tap '😇 I Haven't' (you're safe)
After everyone answers, results show who confessed and who stayed innocent
neverhaveiever.tips: The juicier the confessions, the more fun! Don't be shy. 🙊

truthordrink.name: Truth or Drink
truthordrink.summary: Answer a spicy question truthfully or take a drink!
rules: Each round, one player is in the hot seat with a spicy truth question
The hot seat player chooses: answer truthfully (🗣️ Truth, 150 pts) or skip and drink (🍺 Drink, 50 pts)
Other players watch and react — they can vote whether they believe the answer!
The hot seat rotates each round so everyone gets their turn in the spotlight
truthordrink.tips: The best truths are the ones that surprise everyone! But if it's too spicy... just drink. 🍺

typingrace.name: Monkey Press
typingrace.summary: Race to type a passage faster and more accurately than your friends!
rules: A text passage is shown to all players at the same time
Type the passage as fast and accurately as you can
Your WPM (words per minute) and accuracy are tracked live
When you finish the passage or time runs out, results are compared
Highest WPM with good accuracy wins the round
Multiple rounds are played — total score determines the winner
typingrace.tips: Focus on accuracy first, speed second. Mistakes slow you down more than careful typing!

contexto.name: Contexto
contexto.summary: Guess the secret word by finding semantically related words — lower rank means closer!
rules: A secret word is chosen each round — your goal is to guess it
Type any word and submit — you'll see its rank (how close it is to the secret)
Rank #1 is the most similar word, higher ranks mean less related
Use the ranks to narrow down what the secret word could be
You can use up to 3 hints per round, which reveal ranked words for free
Finding the secret word earns bonus points based on guess count and speed
All players play simultaneously — race to find it first!
contexto.tips: Start with broad category words (animal, food, place) to narrow the theme, then get specific!

mafia.name: Mafia
mafia.summary: A social deduction game — Town vs Mafia. Survive the night and find the killers!
rules: Players are secretly assigned roles: Mafia, Doctor, Detective, or Villager
Night phase: Mafia secretly votes to eliminate someone
Night phase: The Doctor chooses one player to protect from elimination
Night phase: The Detective investigates one player to learn if they're Mafia
Day phase: Everyone discusses who they think the Mafia is
Day phase: Players vote to eliminate a suspect — majority needed
Town wins when all Mafia members are eliminated
Mafia wins when they equal or outnumber the remaining Town players
Eliminated players' roles are revealed
mafia.tips: Pay attention to who's quiet, who accuses too fast, and who changes their story. Trust your gut!
